title: CNC Cycle Time Calculator
description: Calculates the total cycle time for CNC machining operations, including cutting, rapid traverse, tool change, and auxiliary times, with adjustments for material, tool wear, and machine efficiency. Aligns with ISO 9001, Lean Manufacturing, and Six Sigma standards.

inputs.label: Cutting Length
inputs.businessContext: Total length of the tool path in contact with the workpiece. Derived from CAM or drawing.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total length of the tool path in contact with the workpiece. Derived from CAM or drawing.

inputs.label: Feed Rate
inputs.businessContext: Linear feed speed of the cutting tool. Based on material and tool specifications.

businessContext_i18n.en: Linear feed speed of the cutting tool. Based on material and tool specifications.

inputs.label: Spindle Speed
inputs.businessContext: Rotational speed of the spindle. Affects cutting speed and surface finish.

businessContext_i18n.en: Rotational speed of the spindle. Affects cutting speed and surface finish.

inputs.label: Number of Passes
inputs.businessContext: Number of cutting passes required to achieve final geometry.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of cutting passes required to achieve final geometry.

inputs.label: Rapid Traverse Distance
inputs.businessContext: Total distance traveled at rapid traverse (non-cutting moves).

businessContext_i18n.en: Total distance traveled at rapid traverse (non-cutting moves).

inputs.label: Rapid Traverse Rate
inputs.businessContext: Maximum speed of the machine during rapid moves.

businessContext_i18n.en: Maximum speed of the machine during rapid moves.

inputs.label: Tool Change Time
inputs.businessContext: Average time to change a tool, including turret indexing.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average time to change a tool, including turret indexing.

inputs.label: Number of Tool Changes
inputs.businessContext: Total tool changes required for the operation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total tool changes required for the operation.

inputs.label: Material Hardness Factor
inputs.businessContext: Adjusts cutting time based on material machinability. Refer to ISO 3685.

businessContext_i18n.en: Adjusts cutting time based on material machinability. Refer to ISO 3685.

inputs.label: Tool Wear Factor
inputs.businessContext: Multiplier for tool wear. 1.0 = new tool, >1.0 = worn tool. Based on tool life data.

businessContext_i18n.en: Multiplier for tool wear. 1.0 = new tool, >1.0 = worn tool. Based on tool life data.

inputs.label: Machine Efficiency
inputs.businessContext: Overall equipment effectiveness (OEE) factor. Accounts for downtime, speed loss, and quality loss.

businessContext_i18n.en: Overall equipment effectiveness (OEE) factor. Accounts for downtime, speed loss, and quality loss.

inputs.label: Part Loading/Unloading Time
inputs.businessContext: Time to load and unload the workpiece from the machine.

businessContext_i18n.en: Time to load and unload the workpiece from the machine.

inputs.label: Coolant On Time
inputs.businessContext: Time for coolant to activate and stabilize before cutting.

businessContext_i18n.en: Time for coolant to activate and stabilize before cutting.

inputs.label: In-Process Measurement Time
inputs.businessContext: Time for probing or manual measurement during the cycle.

businessContext_i18n.en: Time for probing or manual measurement during the cycle.

rules.message: Feed rate may be too high for the given spindle speed. Consider reducing feed or increasing RPM to avoid chatter.
Rapid traverse distance is high relative to cutting length. Optimize tool path to reduce non-cutting time.
High number of tool changes detected. Consider using combination tools or reducing operations.
Machine efficiency is below 70%. Investigate root causes (downtime, speed loss, defects).

thresholds.message: Cycle time exceeds 10 minutes. Consider process optimization or alternative methods.
Cutting time is less than 30% of total cycle time. High non-cutting overhead detected.

formulas.label: Cutting Time
formulas.description: Total time the tool is engaged in cutting.

formulas.label: Rapid Traverse Time
formulas.description: Total time for non-cutting rapid moves.

formulas.label: Total Tool Change Time
formulas.description: Aggregate time for all tool changes.

formulas.label: Auxiliary Time
formulas.description: Time for part loading, coolant, and measurement.

formulas.label: Raw Cycle Time
formulas.description: Sum of all time components before efficiency adjustment.

formulas.label: Efficiency Adjustment Factor
formulas.description: Factor to account for machine efficiency losses.

formulas.label: Total Cycle Time
formulas.description: Final cycle time per part, adjusted for efficiency.

primary.label: Total Cycle Time
primary.description: The complete cycle time per part, including all operations and efficiency losses.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Non-Cutting Overhead
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Time spent on non-value-added activities (rapid traverse, tool change, auxiliary).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Tool Wear Impact
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Additional time due to worn tool (reduced feed rate or multiple passes).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Material Hardness Impact
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Extra cutting time due to material hardness.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Efficiency Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Time lost due to machine inefficiency (downtime, speed loss, defects).

suggestedActions.label: Optimize Tool Path
suggestedActions.description: Reduce rapid traverse distance by optimizing CAM toolpath. Potential savings: up to 20% of non-cutting time.

suggestedActions.label: Combine Operations
suggestedActions.description: Use multi-functional tools to reduce number of tool changes. Potential savings: up to 15% of cycle time.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Feed Rate
suggestedActions.description: If tool and material allow, increase feed rate to reduce cutting time. Check tool manufacturer specs.

suggestedActions.label: Improve OEE
suggestedActions.description: Implement TPM (Total Productive Maintenance) to reduce downtime and speed losses. Target >85% efficiency.

suggestedActions.label: Use Premium Tooling
suggestedActions.description: Invest in coated or advanced tooling to reduce tool wear factor and allow higher cutting parameters.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Cycle Time
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Cycle time adjusted for data confidence (e.g., based on input variability or historical accuracy).
